title: Expose per-instance active scan counter on /api/health
acceptance_criteria: scan-worker.ts exports a getter getActiveScanCount(): number backed by a module-scoped let activeScanCount = 0 counter.

The counter is incremented immediately before the Promise.race in runScan(), and decremented in a single try/finally wrapping that Promise.race — NOT inside runScanInternal. This ensures the counter is always decremented on all exit paths (success, timeout, uncaught throw).
The counter is named/documented as 'active_scans_this_instance' with a comment noting it is per-process and does not aggregate across multiple container replicas.
/api/health GET response gains a metrics: { active_scans_this_instance: number } field by calling getActiveScanCount().
npm run test passes — existing health endpoint tests still pass; the new field is additive.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
